case_intro.summary: Former tenant Alina claims landlord Boris kept her apartment deposit unfairly after move-out.
case_intro.judge_briefing: Decide whether Boris had grounds to keep the deposit or whether Alina should receive it back.

participants.name: Alina Morozova
participants.relation_to_case: Requests the return of her deposit
participants.public_description: Alina says she left the apartment in good condition and settled all utility payments before move-out.
relationships: Signed the move-out handover with Boris

participants.name: Boris Levin
participants.relation_to_case: Refuses to return the full deposit
participants.public_description: Boris claims the deposit was consumed by wall damage and an unpaid utility bill.

relationships.label: Tenant and landlord
relationships.description: Alina rented Boris's apartment for one year and the dispute is about the return of her deposit.

evidence.title: Lease clause on the deposit
evidence.short_description: The lease says the deposit is returned if the apartment is undamaged and utilities are paid.
evidence.inspection_text: Clause 8 states that the deposit must be returned after a clean move-out inspection and payment of utilities.
evidence.proves: The dispute turns on real damage and utility payment, not on Boris's discretion.

effects.note: Lease terms confirm Boris can keep the deposit only for real damage or unpaid utilities.

evidence.title: Signed move-out report
evidence.short_description: A signed report from the handover inspection.
evidence.inspection_text: The report says: walls intact, no repair costs, both parties signed on the handover date.
evidence.proves: Boris already signed a clean inspection report.

effects.label: A signed clean move-out report exists.

effects.note: Opening the move-out report unlocks a follow-up question for Alina.

dialogue_actions.label: Ask Alina about the utility payment
dialogue_actions.response_text: Alina says she paid the final utility transfer on the morning of move-out and told Boris about it.

effects.label: Alina claims the utility balance was paid before handover.

effects.note: Alina says Boris knew she had sent the final payment.

dialogue_actions.label: Ask Boris why he kept the deposit
dialogue_actions.response_text: Boris says he kept the deposit because the wall needed repainting and Alina had not settled the final utility bill.

effects.note: Boris points to damage and unpaid utilities. His answer unlocks the move-out report.

dialogue_actions.label: Ask Alina about the signed move-out report
dialogue_actions.response_text: Alina says Boris checked the apartment room by room, signed the clean report, and only later changed his story.

effects.label: Boris's damage claim contradicts the signed clean report.

effects.note: The clean report directly challenges Boris's explanation for keeping the deposit.

solution.explanation: Boris's main justification fails because he signed a clean move-out report. Once that contradiction appears, Alina's side is stronger and the deposit should be returned.
key_points: The lease clause limits when Boris can keep the deposit.
Boris claimed wall damage, but the signed move-out report says the apartment was clean.
The contradiction makes Boris's deposit decision unreliable.
